headline: Cybersecurity stocks surging
detail: The headline confirms CrowdStrike and the sector are outperforming today. CRWD is your direct holding and is already up on the year — this tailwind supports holding, but your wide stop at $590 gives it room to run without action needed today.

headline: Supreme Court blocks Trump from firing Fed's Lisa Cook
detail: The ruling reduces immediate fear of Fed political interference, which was a drag on rate-sensitive growth and tech stocks. This is a short-term positive for your entire tech/chip book — markets hate Fed uncertainty, and this removes one layer of it.

headline: Jobs report expected to disappoint this week
detail: Kalshi prediction markets are pricing in a weak payrolls print Friday. A disappointing jobs number could trigger risk-off selling in high-beta tech and chip names — exactly your portfolio. On margin, a Friday sell-off has outsized impact; know your exits on AVGO and PLTR before then.

headline: Wall Street bracing for rate hikes that may never come — growth sectors to gain
detail: If the market reprices away from hike fears, high-multiple tech names like PLTR and growth ETFs like QQQ benefit. This is a slow-burn positive for your book, but it doesn't fix the near-term technical damage already in PLTR and AVGO.
